messages.content:
edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel: Unexpectedly, Anna’s face gleams light and then
speaks out with a kind of loud thundering voice that
frightens everybody, even I am shocked too. Her voice is so
rough and deafening loud that every citizen and guest in the
town hears when Anna says, “You are all having to obey
God the All Mighty, the Absolute and Ultimate Power.”
The audience at the pub have put their hand on their
ears. While, the volume and velocity of her voice have torn
the roof apart and thrown it a hundred yards farther away.
Now everyone is frightened and stunned wherever they
are standing.
The town has come to chaos.

Suddenly, Anna's face radiates an ethereal glow, and a thunderous voice reverberates from her lips, causing an immediate surge of panic among everyone present, myself included. Its roughness and deafening intensity rattles the eardrums of every citizen and visitor in the town, compelling them to heed its commanding authority. "You shall all submit to the omnipotent rule of God, the All Mighty, the Absolute and Ultimate Power," Anna proclaims.

A collective gasp fills the pub as the audience instinctively covers their ears, attempting to shield themselves from the overwhelming resonance. The sheer magnitude and forcefulness of her voice shatters the tranquility, causing the roof to disintegrate and soar a hundred yards away. As chaos ensues, a heavy cloud of fear and astonishment hangs in the air, gripping the hearts of those scattered throughout the town.

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel: Later, we go to the highest point in the city and talk to
the crowds that gather there. I state a brief speech to the
people who have gathered there that their numbers are
increasing at every second.
“You want your miserable life in this Scorching Hell
under suppression and fear or wish to live like a free man
and advance your life condition to a better state!” I speak.
“Those who wish a better life and want to beget
rewarded come to my right side and fight against the dark
queen side by side with me, and those who are content with
their present situation, they have to take one step back and
stay there.”
Most of the crowd come forward and stay on the right
side of me, but of course, with different wishes and
purposes, some came for spoils of war, and some others for
revenge, and a few for advancing to the upper worlds and
for a better life condition.

Later, we ascend to the city's highest point, drawing a crowd of curious onlookers. As the minutes pass, their numbers swell, an undeniable testament to the resonance of our cause. Standing before them, I deliver a brief yet impassioned speech, emphasizing the crucial choice that lies before them.

"Do you wish to persist in this wretched existence, trapped in this Scorching Hell, bound by chains of oppression and fear? Or do you yearn to break free, to live as a liberated being, transcending your present limitations?" I address the crowd, my voice brimming with determined conviction.

"Those who crave a brighter future, those who yearn to be rewarded, gather at my right side. Unite with me, stand shoulder to shoulder, and together, we shall face the dark queen, vanquishing her malevolent reign," I declare, drawing a line in the metaphorical sand.

The majority of the crowd steps forward, aligning themselves at my right side. Among their diverse ranks, motivations vary: some seek the spoils of war, others seek vengeance, and a select few yearn for ascension to higher realms and a more prosperous existence.

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel: After a few days of riding, we with our vast-numbered
army reach the dark queen’s castle. Our military overcomes
the queen’s men, and the castle falls into our hands, but the
wicked queen escapes to the mountains behind her castle.
I send Johan with an army of sorcerers and
necromancers after her. And after a few hours, they bring
her back dead.
I Ask Johan, “What happened there?”
Johan answers, “We tried to capture her alive, but she
was a powerful sorcerer and cast a dangerous spell on our
men, and in a counterattack, she fell off a cliff down and
immediately died.”

After days of relentless riding, our colossal army finally reaches the foreboding stronghold of the dark queen. Engaging in a fierce battle, our forces triumph over the queen's men, swiftly toppling the fortress into our hands. Yet, the wicked queen, elusive as a shadow, manages to elude capture, seeking refuge in the mountains that loom imposingly behind her castle.

To ensure her final defeat, I dispatch Johan at the helm of a fearsome legion of sorcerers and necromancers. The passage of mere hours sees them returning, bearing the lifeless body of the queen. Curiosity gnawing at my mind, I question Johan, seeking answers to the fate that befell our foe.

"What transpired in the mountains?" I inquire, my voice laced with a blend of eagerness and concern.

Johan meets my gaze, his voice laced with a hint of solemnity. "We endeavored to apprehend her alive, but she possessed a formidable mastery of sorcery. Unleashing a perilous spell upon our men, she triggered a ferocious counterattack. In the midst of the chaos, she inadvertently plummeted off a treacherous precipice, sealing her immediate demise."

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel: It takes several months to gather all the lords of different
cities and tribes to create a common ground and rules for
their world.
Finally, I sort them into three categories;
1-Those who do good deed to advance to the upper
world.
2-Those who like where they are but need security and
justice.
3-The minority of violent, and outlaws and rebels.
I organize a high council from the first two groups and
let them elect a governor for four years to run the
underworld on a kind of federation. The government is
based on freedom and independence of each town and tribe,
and they can pursue their own beliefs and traditions,
according to the laws approved by the high council.
A few months later, when I am almost relieved of my
duties and resting on the porch of Johan’s home in his town,
suddenly, Gabriel appears. And immediately, says, “Anna
goes to the second world, you two, (the man and the woman
of John’s first-group) Zack, and Zerac, who were with John
204
from the beginning of his journey, go to the first universe
and you, John, have to come back with me to Earth.”

It takes several arduous months to amass the lords from various realms and tribes, striving to establish a collective foundation and set of principles for their world. Eventually, I succeed in organizing them into distinct categories, each with their own distinct goals and aspirations.

Firstly, there are those who yearn for virtuous deeds, aiming to ascend to the fabled upper world. Secondly, there are those content with their current circumstances yet crave security and justice. Finally, a minority comprises the violent, outlaws, and rebels.

Uniting the first two groups, I form a formidable high council that assumes the responsibility of electing a capable governor. This chosen leader, their term lasting four years, oversees the underworld with a sense of unity and cooperation, forming a federation of sorts. Our government champions individual freedom and independence for each town and tribe, permitting them to pursue their beliefs and traditions within the boundaries of laws approved by the high council.

Months later, as I begin to find respite from my demanding duties, seeking solace on the porch of Johan's humble abode, a sudden apparition disrupts my tranquility. Gabriel materializes in a flash, delivering a message that cuts through the air with urgency.

"Anna must journey to the mysterious second world, accompanied by you, John, along with Zack and Zerac, steadfast companions since the inception of your remarkable odyssey. And as for me, I implore you, John, to return with me to Earth," Gabriel proclaims, his words striking a resounding chord within me.

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel: Gabriel  says: “Anna must journey to the second universe and as for the Johan, Zack and Zerac and those who accompanied the army of john in the great battle with the dark queen forces, they will ascend to the first universe and you, John, have to come back with me to Earth”

With an air of solemnity, Gabriel's voice resonates through the air, bearing the weight of a momentous decree. "Anna's path lies in the uncharted depths of the enigmatic second universe," he proclaims, his words layered with a sense of otherworldly significance. "As for Johan, Zack, and Zerac, faithful stalwarts who stood by John's side in the harrowing battle against the forces of the dark queen, their destiny awaits in the ethereal realms of the first universe. And you, John, are called to return with me to the very realm from whence your extraordinary journey began - Earth itself."

Gabriel's declaration carries a blend of destiny and opportunity, a convergence of fates that propels each individual further along their unique path. The future unfolds, unveiling uncharted realms and challenges that lie beyond the boundaries of their current reality.

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel: I suddenly see myself lying down on the bed of the
emergency room in Missoula hospital, and the doctors and
nurses are giving me CPR and trying hard to bring me back
to life.
Carol is calling me while is crying, “John, John, you
cannot leave me.”
I hear Gabriel says, “John, it is time to go back to your
life. I will meet you later.”
Suddenly, I start breathing and say immediately, “What
has happened and where I am?”
Carol and the doctors don’t let me leave the hospital for
further diagnosis and treatments, although I know there is
nothing wrong with my health.
After a few days of resting in the hospital and going
through many medical processes, I am finally released from
the hospital. And the only diagnosis about my condition was
a minor heart attack caused by too much work and
exhaustion. Of course, this report rejected by the further
examination of the UN hospital research center later on, and
they stated what caused my heart failed for a few seconds a
month ago, is unknown to their knowledge.

In a sudden and disorienting turn of events, my consciousness flickers back into existence, only to find myself lying on the sterile bed of an emergency room in Missoula hospital. The frantic symphony of doctors and nurses surrounds me, their desperate efforts focused on resuscitating me, pulling me back from the precipice of the unknown.

Amidst the chaos, Carol's voice reaches my ears, a plaintive cry laced with tears, echoing through the sterile walls. "John, please don't leave me," she implores, her anguish slicing through the din of medical urgency.

As if in response, Gabriel's serene voice cuts through the tumult, gently beckoning me. "John, it is time to return to the realm of the living. We shall meet again," he assures me, his presence both a comfort and an enigma.

In a moment that blurs between the realms of life and death, I draw a sharp breath, my voice quick to demand answers. "What has happened? Where am I?" I question, seeking to unravel the threads of my fragmented reality.

Carol and the medical team, unwilling to release their grip on my frail hold on life, prevent my immediate departure from the hospital. Their concerns for my well-being intertwine with my own confusion, gradually pushing me towards acceptance of the need for further diagnosis and treatment, despite my inner certainty of my sound health.

Days pass within the sterile confines of the hospital, a blur of rest and medical procedures. Eventually, the chains of their caution are loosened, granting me release. I emerge into the world once more, the lingering diagnosis labeled as a minor heart attack prompted by the weight of excessive work and exhaustion. Yet, this report is swiftly challenged, rejected by the subsequent examinations carried out by the UN hospital research center. Mysteries still cloud the circumstances surrounding those few fleeting seconds of my heart's failure, an enigma that remains beyond the grasp of current medical knowledge.

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel: Carol and the doctors don’t let me leave the hospital for
further diagnosis and treatments, although I know there is
nothing wrong with my health.

In a frustrating twist of events, despite my inner conviction that my health is unblemished, Carol and the doctors refuse to grant me permission to leave the confines of the hospital. Their apprehension holds me captive, denying me the opportunity to seek further diagnosis and treatment.

A burgeoning restlessness tugs at my spirit as each passing day in the sterile hospital environment chips away at my patience. The incessant tests and procedures, though conducted with good intentions, begin to feel like an unnecessary burden on my burgeoning desire for liberation.

I strive to make them understand, to convey the undeniable truth that resides deep within me - there is nothing amiss with my physical well-being. Yet, my pleas fall upon deaf ears, swallowed by a sea of medical caution and bureaucratic protocol.

The corridors echo with unanswered questions, the uncertainty of my situation planting seeds of doubt and frustration within my mind. Despite this internal struggle, I must heed their wishes for the time being, biding my time until a window of opportunity reveals itself.

revise the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel: Carol and the doctors don’t let me leave the hospital for
further diagnosis and treatments, although I know there is
nothing wrong with my health.

To my exasperation, both Carol and the medical professionals stand firm in their decision to confine me to the hospital, depriving me of the freedom to seek additional diagnosis and treatments. Despite my unwavering certainty that my health remains unblemished, they remain resolute in their caution, believing there is more to uncover.

My frustrations mount as each passing day draws out within the restrictive walls of the hospital. The prying tests and ceaseless treatments, however well-intentioned, begin to feel superfluous, prolonging my stay against my will.

I yearn to make them comprehend the certainty that dwells deep within me - an unshakeable conviction that my physical well-being remains intact. Yet, my attempts to persuade them falter, drowned out by a chorus of medical caution and procedural rigidity.

Within the corridors, unanswered queries reverberate, enveloping me in a cloak of uncertainty and restlessness. Despite my internal resistance, I must acquiesce to their wishes for now, yearning for the moment when an opportunity will manifest, granting me the autonomy I seek.

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel: “As for Johan, Zack, and Zerac, and those faithful stalwarts who stood by John’s side in the harrowing battle against the forces of the dark queen, they have an option to ascend and reborn in the first universe. And you, John, are also called to return with me to the Earth.”

"The time has come," Gabriel's voice resonates, brimming with gravity and significance. "Johan, Zack, and Zerac, alongside all those unwavering companions who fought valiantly by John's side in the harrowing battle against the forces of the dark queen, now stand at the precipice of a choice. They hold the opportunity to ascend and be reborn in the wondrous embrace of the first universe." Gabriel's words carry a weight of anticipation and possibility.

"And you, John," he continues, his voice taking on a tone of connection, "are beckoned to journey back with me to Earth, the very realm from which your awe-inspiring tale began." The call to return to that familiar soil, to reconnect with the world left behind, resounds with profound significance.

The option presented to Johan, Zack, Zerac, and my own path stretches before us, initiating a transformative crossroad that may forever alter the trajectory of our existence. Destiny awaits, with the promise of new beginnings and uncharted horizons.

edit and improve the style of the excerpts from my sci-fi fantasy novel: Gabriel talks while I am on my way back to the farm. He
says, “You know that the answers to all the questions that
you might imagine are merely relative to the forms and
arrangements of the seven universes.”
“The Almighty, or the pole is at the center of creation
and rotates tremendously fast while it fluctuates for a
fraction of a second. Something like a whirligig or a
spinner.
“The first bunch of galaxies are in the first orbit (the first
universe) and Earth is a tiny planet in one of its
constellations. These galaxies are nearest to the pole, and
they are orbiting around the pole faster than others. The
second, third, fourth, fifth, sixth, and seventh (universes) go
respectively farther from the pole, and they orbit around the
pole relatively slower. And as farther the orbits (universes)
are from the pole, the number of galaxies in each of them
increases.

As I make my way back to the farm, Gabriel's voice intertwines with the wind, carrying profound knowledge within its ethereal tones. He reveals to me the intricate workings of creation, casting light upon the enigmas that shroud the cosmos.

"John, you must understand that the answers to the questions that plague your mind are intricately tied to the unique forms and arrangements found within the seven diverse universes," Gabriel imparts, his voice both tranquil and authoritative, as if narrating a cosmic poem.

He continues, describing the awe-inspiring dynamics at play within the vast expanse. "Imagine, if you will, an Almighty presence, a celestial pole situated at the very heart of creation. This pole spins with astonishing speed, its fluctuations barely perceptible within a fleeting fraction of a second. It is akin to a mesmerizing whirligig or a fervently whirling spinner."

His words delve deeper into the cosmic structure, unveiling the secrets of our own place within it. "The first cluster of galaxies exists within the first orbit, known as the first universe. Earth, our humble abode, resides as a minuscule planet within one of its constellations. These galaxies, closest to the pole, orbit it with unparalleled speed, surpassing the rhythm of their counterparts. As we venture farther from the pole, the second, third, fourth, fifth, sixth, and seventh universes gradually unfold. Each subsequent universe orbits the pole at a comparably slower pace. Moreover, as the orbits veer from the pole, the number of galaxies within them multiplicatively
